Aufgaben: • Als repräsentative Visitenkarte unserer Auftraggeber (Arxada / DSM usw.) übernehmen Sie die freundliche Begrüssung von Kunden, Gästen und Mitarbeitenden, um eine positive Atmosphäre zu schaffen. • Erfassen und Triagieren der eingehenden Alarme und Treffen der notwendigen Dispositionen. • Überwachung der Gebäude- / Arealsicherheit mithilfe des Kamerasystems und den vorhandenen Sicherheits- / Überwachungssysteme. • Führung des Ereignisjournals und Erstellen von Einsatzrapporten. • Kategorie- und zeitgerechte Erstellung der Badges sowie deren Aktualisierung im Work Day, Zutrittskontrolle und Ausweiserstellsystem. • Unterstützung des Krisenstabes im Ereignisfall. • Bewachung Perimeter. Anforderungsprofil: • Abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung. • Ausbildung im Security Bereich (Security-Coordinator CFPA-E oder gleichwertig). • Erfahrung im Sicherheitsbereich respektive Hintergrund im Sicherheitsdienst (Polizei, Zoll, Grenzwache, Militär, Securitas). • Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift, gute Kenntnisse in Englisch und Französisch. • Werkskentnisse von Vorteil. • Bereitschaft für Schichtarbeit. • Zuverlässige, flexible Person mit hoher Einsatzbereitschaft und Teamfähigkeit. Lonza’s Produkte und Services wirken sich täglich positiv auf Millionen von Menschen aus. Für uns stellt dies nicht nur eine grosse Ehre, sondern auch eine grosse Verantwortung, dar. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erreichen ist für uns genauso wichtig wie unsere Erfolge selbst. Bei Lonza wird Respekt grossgeschrieben und wir schützen sowohl unsere Mitarbeiter als auch unsere Umwelt. Erfolg für uns bedeutet moralisch vertretbarer Fortschritt. Menschen werden Teil der Lonza, um herausfordernde und komplexe Problemstellungen durch kreative und innovative Ansätze im Bereich Life Sciences zu lösen. Im Gegenzug bieten wir die Zufriedenheit, die mit der Verbesserung des Lebens auf der ganzen Welt einhergeht. Die Zufriedenheit, die damit einhergeht, einen sinnvollen Unterschied zu machen. Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.
descriptionByLocale.fr: Lonza Lonza est un leader mondial des sciences de la vie, qui opère sur trois continents. Nous sommes actifs dans la science, mais il n'y a pas de formules magiques pour notre fonctionnement. Notre solution scientifique la plus importante est les employés talentueux qui travaillent ensemble et développent des idées qui aident d'autres entreprises à aider les gens. En retour, nos employés gèrent leur carrière de façon indépendante. Parce que leurs idées, grandes et petites, améliorent le monde. Et c'est le genre de travail que nous voulons partager. ## Le travail Chez Lonza à Viège est à la recherche d'un/e Spécialiste de la sécurité. Tu veux un endroit varié ? Alors appliquez aujourd'hui ! - Oui. En tant que carte d'affaires représentative de nos clients (Arxada / DSM, etc.), prendre en charge l'accueil amical des clients, des invités et des employés pour créer une atmosphère positive. - Capturer et trier les alarmes entrantes et répondre aux dispositions nécessaires. - Surveiller la sécurité du bâtiment/de la zone en utilisant le système de caméras et les systèmes de sécurité/de surveillance existants. - Guidez le journal de l'événement et créez des rapports d'application. - La création en classe et en temps opportun des badges ainsi que leur mise à jour lors de la journée de travail, le contrôle d'accès et le système d'identification. - Soutien de l'équipe de crise dans l'événement. - Le périmètre de garde. ## Requisiti - Formation professionnelle complète. - Formation en sécurité (coordonnateur de la sécurité CFPA-E ou équivalent). - Expérience dans le domaine de la sécurité ou dans les services de sécurité (police, douane, garde frontière, militaire, Securitas). - Très bonne connaissance allemande de la parole et de l'écriture, bonne connaissance en anglais et en français. - Des scènes d'avantage. - Prêt pour le travail posté. - Une personne fiable et flexible avec un engagement élevé et une capacité d'équipe. Les produits et services de Cosa offriamo Lonza ont un impact positif sur des millions de personnes chaque jour. Pour nous, ce n'est pas seulement un grand honneur, mais aussi une grande responsabilité. À mesure que nous atteignons nos résultats commerciaux, il est tout aussi important pour nous que nos succès eux-mêmes. À Lonza, le respect est donné et nous protégeons nos employés et notre environnement. Le succès pour nous signifie un progrès moralement raisonnable. Les gens font partie de la Lonza pour résoudre des problèmes complexes et difficiles grâce à des approches créatives et novatrices des sciences de la vie. En retour, nous offrons la satisfaction associée à l'amélioration de la vie dans le monde entier. La satisfaction associée à faire une différence significative. ## Contatto Lonza est un leader mondial dans la fabrication de produits pharmaceutiques et de biotechnologies. La société exploite d'importantes installations de production à Viège (Valais), Bâle et Stein (Argovie), Suisse.

jobs.title: Group Leader Analytical Development (Small Molecules) 80-100% (m/f/d)

titleByLocale.it: Leader del Gruppo Sviluppo analitico (molecole minori) 80-100% (m/f/d)

jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

In this senior leadership role, you will lead an interdisciplinary Analytical Development team responsible for the implementation, development,  optimization, and validation of analytical methods for  small‑molecule products in a GMP‑regulated environment. In addition, the team provides analytical expertise and measurement support to process development and manufacturing teams, advising on analytical techniques, data interpretation, and method selection.
You will ensure that people, capabilities, and assets consistently deliver high‑quality analytical services, particularly chromatography techniques to internal and external customers, in line with agreed timelines, cost targets, and safety standards.

What you will get

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi 

What you will do:

• Provide scientific and disciplinary leadership for a team of approximately

• 30 development scientists, project leaders, and laboratory technicians

• Coordinates and optimizes the allocation of resources (e.g. personnel, equipment, laboratories, etc) to meet project needs and customer expectations. 

• Oversee the development, validation, transfer of analytical methods, and support of process development and manufacturing teams with a strong focus on chromatographic techniques

• Ensure full compliance with GMP, quality standards, and safety regulations across all development and production support activities

• Manage project portfolios, budgets, timelines, and resource allocation to meet customer and business objectives

• Act as a technical expert and key interface for internal stakeholders and external customers, including participation in audits and inspections

• Drive continuous improvement, innovation, and operational excellence in analytical and chemical development processes

What we are looking for:

• PhD or MSc in Chemistry, Analytical Chemistry, Pharmacy, or Chemical Engineering

• More than 10 years of industrial experience in chemical and/or analytical development within a GMP environment

• Strong expertise in chromatographic methods (e.g. HPLC/UPLC, LC‑MS, GC) applied to process understanding and optimization

• You demonstrate creative problem‑solving, active listening, and strong communication skills, enabling effective cross‑functional collaboration and interdisciplinary teamwork.

• Demonstrated leadership experience managing and developing multidisciplinary teams in an industrial setting

• Solid understanding of business processes, cost control, and customer‑focused project execution

• Fluency in English; German proficiency or strong willingness to learn German

About Lonza

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? 
Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Scientist QC Microbiology 80-100%
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Stein, AG, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

Join us as Scientist in our QC Microbiology department and become part of an exciting growth project for a new sterile drug product facility! Check out your career at Drug Product Services (DPS)

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.

• Benefits in Stein: https://bit.ly/42DlBDk 

What you’ll do:

• Routine execution and coordination of microbiological testing including environmental monitoring, bacterial endotoxins-, bioburden- and sterility testing etc.

• Adhere to GMP regulations to ensure flawless operations

• Perform, develop, establish and improve microbiological testing procedures, e.g. method suitability testing and provide training for test methods

• Support microbiology-related projects, such as implementation of new methods

• Ensure appropriate maintenance of equipment, safety and health protection following laboratory and safety instructions and all cGMP regulations

• Train analytical methods and/or corresponding instruments

What we’re looking for:

• Education as Lab Technician, Bachelor or equivalent in Microbiology or Biology, Pharmaceutical Sciences or related fields

• Working experience in microbiological testing, preferably in pharmaceutical industry working under cGMP Regulations

• Sound working knowledge in all (and in-depth knowledge in at least one) relevant microbiological test types (environmental monitoring, endotoxin, bioburden and sterility testing)

• Knowledge with automated systems (e.g. Growth direct, Pyrotec Pro) is an asset

• Excellent communication skills

• Fluency in written and spoken English, German is an asset

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.
Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Head Visual Inspection 80-100%
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Stein, AG, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

In this role you will have the opportunity to lead a business‑critical visual inspection and container closure integrity testing team at the heart of Drug Product Services. With full technical ownership across automated, semi‑automated, and manual visual inspection processes, you will partner closely with customers, health authorities, and cross‑functional teams to deliver safe, compliant, and high‑quality outcomes. You will lead and develop a fast‑growing expert team, driving innovation and successfully scaling next‑generation inspection technologies as part of an exciting growth project.
Check out Your Career in Drug Product  Services at Lonza !

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• A highly collaborative environment that fosters teamwork and inclusion.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.
Benefits in Stein: https://bit.ly/42DlBDk

What you will do:

• Manage a team of a Vision Engineers, Process Experts and Operators with responsibility for all aspects of people leadership such as development, coaching, hiring and disciplinary topics. Maintain a culture of empowerment and innovation, trust, high performance and change readiness.

• Technical system ownership: Provide scientific leadership to the team and other functions to ensure visual inspection processes are executed timely, cost effective and with the highest quality. Proactively anticipate, evaluate and resolve technical challenges and enables innovation.

• Responsible for operative takeover of the automated, semi-automated,  manual visual inspection and  container closure integrity testing

• Manage the customer interface in technical client visits and adapt technical capabilities in order to meet the customer requirements. Represent the facility during regulatory inspections and audits of clients and health authorities.

• Accountable for safety, compliance and regulatory aspects. Preparation of risk analysis and all relevant procedures to ensure regulatory compliance and achieve business goals.

• Support of the project team regarding complex topics by use of scientific and statistical knowledge.

• Ensure and manage the interfaces between CAPEX team, Manufacturing, Plant Engineering, Automation, Support, QC and QA. Collaborate with functions in the project and across Drug Product Services, especially the existing drug product facilities)

• Support the Senior Director Operational Lead of one of our largest growth project (Dolphin) in developing, prioritizing and achieving goals with respect to the project and overall DPS (Drug Product Services) goals. Ensure a good information exchange from the management level to the organization and the other way around through regularly team meetings and clear communication.

What we are looking for:

• Extensive experience in visual inspection of parenteral drug product (specifically vials and syringes) in a GMP environment is a must. Broad experience with various different visual inspection systems such as manual, semi-automatic or AVI is an advantage.

• Team leadership experience is a prerequisite

• Experience with audits of health authorities

• Fluency in English is a must (fluency in German is an advantage) with strong communication skills with stakeholders from various functions and levels.

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? 
Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

descriptionByLocale.it: Descrizione La posizione attuale di questo lavoro è in Stein, AG, Svizzera. L'assistenza di trasferimento è disponibile per i candidati idonei e le loro famiglie, se necessario. ## Mansioni - Gestire un team di ingegneri di visione, esperti di processo e operatori con la responsabilità di tutti gli aspetti della leadership delle persone come lo sviluppo, coaching, l'assunzione e temi disciplinari. Mantenere una cultura di empowerment e innovazione, fiducia, alte prestazioni e disponibilità di cambiamento. - Proprietà del sistema tecnico: Fornire leadership scientifica al team e altre funzioni per garantire che i processi di ispezione visiva siano eseguiti puntuale, conveniente e con la massima qualità. Rappresentare la struttura durante le ispezioni regolamentari e gli audit dei clienti e delle autorità sanitarie. - Contabile per aspetti di sicurezza, conformità e regolamentazione. Preparazione dell'analisi del rischio e di tutte le procedure pertinenti per garantire la conformità normativa e raggiungere gli obiettivi aziendali. - Supporto del team di progetto per argomenti complessi utilizzando conoscenze scientifiche e statistiche. - Assicurare e gestire le interfacce tra il team CAPEX, Manufacturing, Plant Engineering, Automation, Support, QC e QA. Collaborare con le funzioni del progetto e attraverso i Servizi di Prodotto Droga, in particolare le strutture esistenti di prodotto farmaceutico) - Sostenere il Direttore Senior Lead Operativo di uno dei nostri più grandi progetti di crescita (Dolphin) nello sviluppo, la priorità e il raggiungimento degli obiettivi rispetto al progetto e agli obiettivi globali DPS (Drug Product Services). Assicurare un buon scambio di informazioni dal livello di gestione all'organizzazione e l'altro modo in giro attraverso riunioni di team regolari e comunicazione chiara. ## Requisiti - Ampia esperienza nell'ispezione visiva del prodotto farmaceutico parenterale (specificamente fiale e siringhe) in un ambiente GMP è un must. - Ampia esperienza con diversi sistemi di ispezione visiva come manuale, semiautomatico o AVI è un vantaggio. - No. L'esperienza di leadership del team è un prerequisito - Esperienza con audit delle autorità sanitarie - La fluidità in inglese è un must (la potenza in tedesco è un vantaggio) con forti capacità di comunicazione con gli stakeholder di varie funzioni e livelli. # Cosa# offrendo - Una carriera agile e una cultura del lavoro dinamica. - Un ambiente altamente collaborativo che favorisce il lavoro di squadra e l'inclusione. - No. Un luogo di lavoro inclusivo ed etico. - Programmi di compensazione che riconoscono alte prestazioni. Oltre a uno stipendio competitivo, si può aspettare numerosi lifestyle, famiglia e benefici per il tempo libero. La nostra lista completa di benefici locali su misura può essere trovata di seguito. - Vantaggi in Stein: https://bit.ly/42DlBDk ## Contatto Pronto a modellare il futuro delle scienze della vita? Applichi subito. ## Azienda At Lonza, la nostra gente Ã ̈ la nostra massima forza. Con 30+ siti in cinque continenti, i nostri team collegati a livello globale lavorano insieme ogni giorno per produrre i farmaci di domani. I nostri valori fondamentali di collaborazione, responsabilità, eccellenza, passione e integrità riflettono chi siamo e come lavoriamo insieme. Le idee di tutti, grandi o piccole, hanno il potenziale di migliorare milioni di vite, e questo è il tipo di lavoro di cui vogliamo che tu faccia parte. L'innovazione prospera quando le persone di tutti gli sfondi portano le loro prospettive uniche al tavolo. A Lonza, apprezziamo la diversità e ci impegniamo a creare un ambiente inclusivo per tutti i dipendenti. Se siete pronti ad aiutare a trasformare le idee innovative dei nostri clienti in terapie valide, non vediamo l'ora di accogliervi a bordo. Lonza è leader globale nella produzione di farmaci e biotecnologie. L'azienda gestisce importanti stabilimenti produttivi a Visp (Valais), Basilea e Stein (Aargau), Svizzera.

Avec plus de 30 sites sur cinq continents, nos équipes connectées à l'échelle mondiale travaillent tous les jours pour fabriquer les médicaments de demain. Nos valeurs fondamentales de collaboration, de responsabilisation, d'excellence, de passion et d'intégrité reflètent qui nous sommes et comment nous travaillons ensemble. Les idées de tout le monde, grandes ou petites, ont le potentiel d'améliorer des millions de vies, et c'est le genre de travail auquel nous voulons que vous participiez. L'innovation prospère lorsque les gens de tous les horizons apportent leurs points de vue uniques à la table. Chez Lonza, nous apprécions la diversité et nous nous engageons à créer un environnement inclusif pour tous les employés. Si vous êtes prêt à aider nos clients à transformer des idées révolutionnaires en thérapies viables, nous nous réjouissons de vous accueillir à bord. Lonza est un leader mondial dans la fabrication de produits pharmaceutiques et de biotechnologies. La société exploite d'importantes installations de production à Viège (Valais), Bâle et Stein (Argovie), Suisse.

jobs.title: Operator / Senior Operator
jobs.description:
Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across five continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of.
Lonza is a global leader in life sciences, operating on three continents. While we are driven by science, there is no magic formula for how we approach our work. Our most valuable scientific solution is our dedicated people who collaborate and develop ideas that help other companies improve lives. In return, our employees manage their careers independently, as their ideas—whether big or small—make the world a better place. This is the kind of work we want to be part of.
A new production plant in the field of biotechnological production is being built at the Visp site. This is an exciting time to join our team, whether you’re a seasoned professional or just starting your career. We’re seeking motivated and resilient individuals to play a key role in vaccine production—both during the project phase and as we transition to routine operations.
Join us as an Operator – Opportunities for All Experience Levels!

What you’ll do:
during the project phase:

• Support the project team in defining and setting up innovative vaccine production processes.

• Collaborate on the qualification and validation of new production areas and processes.

in routine production

• Adhere to relevant SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures) and regulations (e.g., hygiene concept, safety regulations)

• Independently operate production plants and equipment, including preparation, operation, and post-processing, following SOPs.

• Perform microbiological environmental monitoring, in-process controls (IPC), and sample taking

• Monitor production processes, detect and report potential issues, and actively participate in continuous improvement processes

• Conduct GMP-compliant work and timely documentation of activities (e.g., computer systems (MES) or log books)

• Handle, clean, store, and inspect equipment

What we’re looking for:

• We welcome candidates from all experience levels, whether you’re a seasoned expert or a career changer with relevant qualifications. If you’re enthusiastic about contributing to cutting-edge vaccine production, we’d love to hear from you

• for Entry-Level Candidates: Completed apprenticeship in pharmaceutical, chemical, or biotechnological fields, or a relevant university degree

• for Experienced Professionals: Several years of experience in pharmaceutical production, particularly in GMP and cleanroom environments, is a plus.

• Safety awareness

• Fluent in German and/or English

• Willingness to work shifts (4 shifts)

• Ability to work in a team, reliability, and a high degree of personal responsibility

• Flexibility

Lonza's products and services positively impact millions of people every day. For us, this is not only a great honor but also a significant responsibility. How we achieve our business results is as important to us as the results themselves. At Lonza, respect is paramount, and we protect both our employees and our environment. For us, success means morally justifiable progress.
Lonza is joined by people who take on challenges.
Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

Inclusion, Diversity & Belonging (IDB)
Inclusion is one of Lonza’s four core values, alongside integrity, innovation and initiative. We understand the value of our differences and the benefits they bring to our company, community and culture. We also recognize the value of every person’s contribution, regardless of characteristics, preferences and beliefs – including race, color, ethnicity, sexual orientation, gender identity and religion.

Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically.
People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a meaningful difference.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

descriptionByLocale.it: Descrizione Oggi, Lonza è leader globale nelle scienze della vita che operano in cinque continenti. Mentre lavoriamo nella scienza, non c'è formula magica per come lo facciamo. La nostra più grande soluzione scientifica è la gente di talento che lavora insieme, ideando idee che aiutano le imprese ad aiutare le persone. In cambio, lasciamo che la nostra gente possieda le loro carriere. Le loro idee, grandi e piccole, migliorano veramente il mondo. E questo è il tipo di lavoro di cui vogliamo essere parte. Lonza è leader globale nelle scienze della vita, operante in tre continenti. Mentre siamo guidati dalla scienza, non c'è formula magica per come ci avviciniamo al nostro lavoro. La nostra soluzione scientifica più preziosa è la nostra gente dedicata che collabora e sviluppa idee che aiutano altre aziende a migliorare la vita. In cambio, i nostri dipendenti gestiscono le loro carriere in modo indipendente, come le loro idee, sia grandi che piccole, rendono il mondo un posto migliore. Questo è il tipo di lavoro che vogliamo fare. Un nuovo impianto di produzione nel campo della produzione biotecnologica è in fase di costruzione presso il sito Visp. Questo è un momento emozionante per unirsi alla nostra squadra, se sei un professionista esperto o appena iniziare la tua carriera. Siamo alla ricerca di individui motivati e resilienti a svolgere un ruolo chiave nella produzione di vaccini, sia durante la fase di progetto che come transizione alle operazioni di routine. Unisciti a noi come Operatore – Opportunità per tutti i livelli di esperienza! ## Mansioni - durante la fase di progetto: - Sostenere il team di progetto nella definizione e nella creazione di processi innovativi di produzione di vaccini. - Collaborare alla qualificazione e alla validazione di nuove aree e processi produttivi. - nella produzione di routine - Attenti alle SOP pertinenti (Procedura di funzionamento standard) e ai regolamenti (ad esempio, concetto di igiene, regolamenti di sicurezza) - Indipendentemente operare impianti e attrezzature di produzione, tra cui preparazione, funzionamento e post-elaborazione, seguendo le SOP. - Eseguire il monitoraggio microbiologico dell'ambiente, i controlli in-process (IPC), e l'assunzione del campione - Monitorare i processi di produzione, rilevare e segnalare potenziali problemi, e partecipare attivamente ai processi di miglioramento continuo - Condurre il lavoro conforme a GMP e la documentazione tempestiva delle attività (ad esempio, sistemi informatici (MES) o libri di registro) - Maniglia, pulizia, negozio e ispezione attrezzature ## Requisiti - Accogliamo i candidati da tutti i livelli di esperienza, sia che tu sia un esperto esperto esperto esperto esperto o un cambiamento di carriera con qualifiche rilevanti. Se siete entusiasti di contribuire alla produzione di vaccini all'avanguardia, ci piacerebbe sentire da voi - per i candidati Entry-Level: apprendistato completo in campi farmaceutici, chimici o biotecnologici, o una laurea rilevante - per i professionisti esperti: Diversi anni di esperienza nella produzione farmaceutica, in particolare negli ambienti GMP e cleanroom, è un plus. - Sensibilizzazione alla sicurezza - Fluente in tedesco e/o inglese - Volontà ai turni di lavoro (4 turni) - Capacità di lavorare in team, affidabilità e alto grado di responsabilità personale - Flessibilità # # Cosa offrono - I prodotti e i servizi di Lonza influenzano positivamente milioni di persone ogni giorno. Per noi, questo non è solo un grande onore, ma anche una responsabilità significativa. Come raggiungere i nostri risultati aziendali è importante per noi come i risultati stessi. A Lonza, il rispetto è fondamentale, e proteggiamo sia i nostri dipendenti che il nostro ambiente. Per noi, il successo significa progresso moralmente giustificabile. - Lonza è unita a persone che affrontano sfide. - L'assistenza di trasferimento è disponibile per i candidati idonei e le loro famiglie, se necessario. ## Inclusione, Diversity & Belonging (IDB) - L'inclusione è uno dei quattro valori fondamentali di Lonza, insieme all'integrità, all'innovazione e all'iniziativa. Comprendiamo il valore delle nostre differenze e i benefici che portano alla nostra azienda, comunità e cultura. Riconosciamo anche il valore del contributo di ogni persona, indipendentemente dalle caratteristiche, dalle preferenze e dalle credenze – tra cui razza, colore, etnia, orientamento sessuale, identità di genere e religione. ## Contatto - Ogni giorno i prodotti e i servizi di Lonza hanno un impatto positivo su milioni di persone. Per noi, questo non è solo un grande privilegio, ma anche una grande responsabilità. Come raggiungere i nostri risultati aziendali è altrettanto importante quanto i risultati stessi. A Lonza, rispettiamo e proteggiamo la nostra gente e il nostro ambiente. Qualsiasi successo ottenuto non è affatto successo se non raggiunto eticamente. - La gente viene a Lonza per la sfida e la creatività di risolvere problemi complessi e sviluppare nuove idee nelle scienze della vita. In cambio, offriamo la soddisfazione che viene con migliorare le vite in tutto il mondo. La soddisfazione che viene con fare una differenza significativa. - Lonza è leader globale nella produzione di farmaci e biotecnologie. L'azienda gestisce importanti stabilimenti produttivi a Visp (Valais), Basilea e Stein (Aargau), Svizzera.

descriptionByLocale.de: Heute ist Lonza weltweit führend in Life Sciences, die auf fünf Kontinenten tätig sind. Während wir in der Wissenschaft arbeiten, gibt es keine magische Formel, wie wir es tun. Unsere größte wissenschaftliche Lösung ist talentierte Menschen, die zusammenarbeiten und Ideen entwickeln, die Unternehmen helfen, Menschen zu helfen. Im Gegenzug lassen wir unsere Leute ihre Karriere besitzen. Ihre Ideen, groß und klein, wirklich verbessern die Welt. Und das ist die Art von Arbeit, von der wir teilnehmen wollen. Lonza ist weltweit führend in Life Sciences, die auf drei Kontinenten tätig sind. Während wir von der Wissenschaft angetrieben werden, gibt es keine magische Formel, wie wir unsere Arbeit nähern. Unsere wertvollste wissenschaftliche Lösung ist unsere engagierten Menschen, die Ideen entwickeln, die anderen Unternehmen helfen, das Leben zu verbessern. Im Gegenzug verwalten unsere Mitarbeiter ihre Karriere selbständig, da ihre Ideen – ob groß oder klein – die Welt zu einem besseren Ort machen. Das ist die Art von Arbeit, von der wir teilnehmen wollen. Am Standort Visp wird eine neue Produktionsanlage im Bereich der biotechnologischen Produktion errichtet. Dies ist eine aufregende Zeit, um unserem Team beizutreten, ob Sie ein erfahrener Profi sind oder einfach nur Ihre Karriere beginnen. Wir suchen motivierte und widerstandsfähige Individuen, um eine Schlüsselrolle bei der Impfstoffproduktion zu spielen – sowohl während der Projektphase als auch beim Übergang zu Routineoperationen. Begleiten Sie uns als Betreiber – Möglichkeiten für alle Erfahrungsstufen! Mansioni unterstützt das Projektteam dabei, innovative Impfstoffproduktionsprozesse zu definieren und zu etablieren. - Zusammenarbeit bei der Qualifizierung und Validierung neuer Produktionsbereiche und Prozesse. - in der routinemäßigen Produktion - Bei relevanten SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures) und Verordnungen (z.B. Hygienekonzept, Sicherheitsvorschriften) - Unabhängig arbeitende Produktionsanlagen und -ausrüstung, einschließlich Vorbereitung, Betrieb und Nachbearbeitung, nach SOPs. - Durchführung mikrobiologischer Umweltüberwachung, Prozesskontrollen (IPC) und Probenentnahme - Überwachung von Produktionsprozessen, Erkennung und Meldung potenzieller Probleme und aktive Teilnahme an kontinuierlichen Verbesserungsprozessen - Durchführung GMP-konformer Arbeit und rechtzeitige Dokumentation von Aktivitäten (z.B. Computersysteme (MES) oder Logbüchern) - Handle, clean, store und inspect equipment Wir begrüßen Kandidaten aus allen Erfahrungsstufen, ob Sie ein erfahrener Experte oder Karrierewechsler mit relevanten Qualifikationen sind. Wenn Sie begeistert sind, zur hochmodernen Impfstoffproduktion beizutragen, würden wir gerne von Ihnen hören - für Entry-Level-Kandidaten: Vollständige Ausbildung in pharmazeutischen, chemischen oder biotechnologischen Bereichen oder ein relevanter Hochschulabschluss - für Experienced Professionals: Mehrere Jahre Erfahrung in der pharmazeutischen Produktion, insbesondere in GMP- und Reinraumumgebungen, ist ein Plus. - Sicherheitsbewusstsein - Fluent in Deutsch und/oder Englisch - Willingness to workshifts (4 Verschiebungen) - Fähigkeit, in einem Team zu arbeiten, Zuverlässigkeit und ein hohes Maß an persönlicher Verantwortung - Flexibilität Cosa offriamo - Lonzas Produkte und Dienstleistungen beeinflussen jeden Tag positiv Millionen von Menschen. Für uns ist dies nicht nur eine große Ehre, sondern auch eine bedeutende Verantwortung. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erzielen, ist uns ebenso wichtig wie die Ergebnisse selbst. Bei Lonza ist Respekt von größter Bedeutung und wir schützen unsere Mitarbeiter und unsere Umwelt. Erfolg bedeutet für uns moralisch gerechtfertigte Fortschritte. - Lonza wird von Menschen begleitet, die Herausforderungen angehen. - Umsiedlungshilfe ist bei förderungsberechtigten Bewerbern und ihren Familien, falls erforderlich, verfügbar. Inclusion, Diversity & Belonging (IDB) - Inclusion ist einer der vier Kernwerte von Lonza, neben Integrität, Innovation und Initiative. Wir verstehen den Wert unserer Unterschiede und die Vorteile, die sie unserem Unternehmen, unserer Gemeinschaft und unserer Kultur bringen. Wir erkennen auch den Wert des Beitrags jeder Person, unabhängig von Merkmalen, Präferenzen und Überzeugungen – einschließlich Rasse, Farbe, Ethnizität, sexuelle Orientierung, Geschlechtsidentität und Religion. Contatto - Die Produkte und Dienstleistungen von Lonza haben jeden Tag positive Auswirkungen auf Millionen von Menschen. Für uns ist dies nicht nur ein großes Privileg, sondern auch eine große Verantwortung. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erzielen, ist ebenso wichtig wie die Leistungen selbst. In Lonza respektieren und schützen wir unser Volk und unsere Umwelt. Jeder Erfolg, den wir erreichen, ist überhaupt kein Erfolg, wenn nicht ethisch erreicht. - Die Menschen kommen nach Lonza für die Herausforderung und Kreativität, komplexe Probleme zu lösen und neue Ideen in den Life Sciences zu entwickeln. Im Gegenzug bieten wir die Zufriedenheit, die mit der Verbesserung des Lebens auf der ganzen Welt kommt. Die Zufriedenheit, die mit einem sinnvollen Unterschied kommt. - Lonza ist weltweit führend in der Pharma- und Biotech-Produktion. Das Unternehmen betreibt große Produktionsstätten in Visp (Valais), Basel und Stein (Aargau), Schweiz.
descriptionByLocale.fr: Descrizione Aujourd'hui, Lonza est un leader mondial des sciences de la vie opérant sur cinq continents. Pendant que nous travaillons dans la science, il n'y a pas de formule magique pour comment nous le faisons. Notre plus grande solution scientifique est de travailler ensemble avec des gens talentueux, en concevant des idées qui aident les entreprises à aider les gens. En échange, nous laissons notre peuple posséder sa carrière. Leurs idées, grandes et petites, améliorent véritablement le monde. Et c'est le genre de travail dont nous voulons faire partie. Lonza est un leader mondial des sciences de la vie, opérant sur trois continents. Alors que nous sommes animés par la science, il n'y a pas de formule magique pour notre approche de notre travail. Notre solution scientifique la plus précieuse est notre personnel dévoué qui collabore et développe des idées qui aident d'autres entreprises à améliorer la vie. En retour, nos employés gèrent leur carrière de façon indépendante, car leurs idées, grandes ou petites, font du monde un meilleur endroit. C'est le genre de travail dont nous voulons faire partie. Une nouvelle usine de production dans le domaine de la production biotechnologiques est en construction sur le site de Viège. C'est un moment passionnant pour rejoindre notre équipe, que vous soyez un professionnel chevronné ou tout simplement commencer votre carrière. Nous cherchons des individus motivés et résilients à jouer un rôle clé dans la production de vaccins, tant pendant la phase du projet que pendant la transition vers des opérations de routine. Rejoignez-nous en tant qu'opérateur – Opportunités pour tous les niveaux d'expérience! ## Mansioni - pendant la phase du projet : - Appuyer l'équipe du projet dans la définition et la mise en place de processus de production de vaccins innovants. - Collaborer à la qualification et à la validation de nouveaux domaines et procédés de production. - dans la production courante - Adhérer aux SOP pertinentes (procédures d'exploitation normalisées) et aux règlements (p. ex., concept d'hygiène, règlements de sécurité) - Utiliser des installations et des équipements de production indépendants, y compris la préparation, l'exploitation et la post-traitement, conformément aux SOP. - Effectuer une surveillance microbiologique de l'environnement, des contrôles en cours de fabrication et des prélèvements d'échantillons - Surveiller les processus de production, détecter et signaler les problèmes potentiels et participer activement aux processus d'amélioration continue - Effectuer des travaux conformes aux BPF et documenter en temps opportun les activités (p. ex. systèmes informatiques (MES) ou carnets de bord) - Manipulation, nettoyage, stockage et inspection de l'équipement ## Requisiti - Nous accueillons les candidats de tous les niveaux d'expérience, que vous soyez un expert chevronné ou un changement de carrière avec des qualifications pertinentes. Si vous êtes enthousiaste à l'idée de contribuer à la production de vaccins de pointe, nous aimerions avoir de vos nouvelles - pour les candidats de niveau d'entrée: Apprentissage complété dans les domaines pharmaceutiques, chimiques ou biotechnologiques, ou un diplôme universitaire pertinent - pour les professionnels expérimentés: Plusieurs années d'expérience dans la production pharmaceutique, particulièrement dans les BPF et les environnements propres, est un plus. - Sensibilisation à la sécurité - Fluent en allemand et/ou en anglais - Volonté de travailler par quarts (4 quarts) - Capacité de travailler en équipe, fiabilité et responsabilité personnelle élevée - Flexibilité ## Cosa offriamo - Les produits et services de Lonza ont un impact positif sur des millions de personnes chaque jour. Pour nous, ce n'est pas seulement un grand honneur, mais aussi une responsabilité importante. La façon dont nous obtenons nos résultats commerciaux est aussi importante pour nous que les résultats eux-mêmes. À Lonza, le respect est primordial et nous protégeons nos employés et notre environnement. Pour nous, le succès signifie un progrès moralement justifiable. - Lonza est rejoint par des gens qui prennent des défis. - Une aide à la réinstallation est offerte aux candidats admissibles et à leur famille, au besoin. ## Inclusion, diversité et appartenance (IDB) - L'inclusion est l'une des quatre valeurs fondamentales de Lonza, à côté de l'intégrité, de l'innovation et de l'initiative. Nous comprenons la valeur de nos différences et les avantages qu'elles apportent à notre entreprise, à notre communauté et à notre culture. Nous reconnaissons également la valeur de la contribution de chaque personne, quelles que soient ses caractéristiques, ses préférences et ses croyances, y compris la race, la couleur, l'ethnicité, l'orientation sexuelle, l'identité de genre et la religion. ## Contatto - Chaque jour, les produits et services de Lonza ont un impact positif sur des millions de personnes. Pour nous, ce n'est pas seulement un grand privilège, mais aussi une grande responsabilité. La façon dont nous obtenons nos résultats commerciaux est tout aussi importante que les réalisations elles-mêmes. À Lonza, nous respectons et protégeons notre peuple et notre environnement. Tout succès que nous obtenons n'est pas du tout un succès sinon un succès éthique. - Les gens viennent à Lonza pour le défi et la créativité de résoudre des problèmes complexes et de développer de nouvelles idées dans les sciences de la vie. En retour, nous offrons la satisfaction qui vient avec l'amélioration de la vie partout dans le monde. La satisfaction qui vient avec faire une différence significative. - Lonza est un leader mondial dans la fabrication pharmaceutique et biotechnologique. La société exploite d'importantes installations de production à Viège (Valais), Bâle et Stein (Argovie), Suisse.

jobs.title: Bioprocess Engineer
jobs.description:
Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across three continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of. 

 

The Bioprocess Engineer (BPE) provides technical and operational support for GMP manufacturing operations in Buffer & Media Preparation (BMS) Acting as the process Subject Matter Expert (SME), the BPE ensures safe, compliant, and reliable execution of manufacturing activities while driving standardization, continuous improvement, and operational readiness.

The role works closely with Manufacturing, QA, MSAT, Engineering, and Automation, and maintains a strong shop‑floor presence during routine operations, PPQ, and critical GMP campaigns.

Key Responsibilities
Operational & Technical Support

• Provide hands‑on technical support to manufacturing teams during GMP operations and PPQ campaigns

• Act as process SME for assigned equipment, systems, and manufacturing activities

• Support tier meetings and structured escalation of operational risks and issues

• Participate in on‑call (pikett) support as required

Quality & GMP Compliance

• Lead or support deviation investigations, root cause analysis, and action closure

• Ensure operations comply with cGMP, data integrity, and safety requirements

• Author and review GMP documentation (SOPs, protocols, risk assessments, changes)

• Support regulatory inspections and audit readiness activities

Process Ownership & Improvement

• Own assigned processes, recipes, and equipment from readiness through routine use

• Drive standardization and process robustness across BMS

• Support commissioning, qualification, and validation activities as manufacturing SME

• Identify and implement continuous improvement opportunities (Lean / Six Sigma)

Execution Readiness & Collaboration

• Partner with Production and Planning to ensure material, equipment, and recipe readiness

• Proactively identify and manage execution risks impacting batch success and schedule adherence

• Collaborate cross‑functionally to resolve technical and compliance issues efficiently

Required Qualifications

• BSc or MSc in Biotechnology, Chemical Engineering, Bioprocess Engineering, or related field

• Experience in GMP biopharmaceutical manufacturing (large‑scale operations preferred)

• Strong understanding of BMS, USP, DSP, and/or single‑use systems

• Working knowledge of GMP documentation, deviations, and investigations

Key Competencies

• Strong shop‑floor presence and problem‑solving capability

• Structured, detail‑oriented, and quality‑focused mindset

• Excellent communication and cross‑functional collaboration skills

• Ability to prioritize and perform in a dynamic manufacturing environment

What We Offer

• Work in a state‑of‑the‑art large‑scale manufacturing environment

• High‑impact role supporting PPQ and commercial production

• Collaborative, safety‑first culture with strong development opportunities

Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically.  

 

People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a meaningful difference.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

descriptionByLocale.it: Descrizione Oggi, Lonza è leader globale nelle scienze della vita che operano in tre continenti. Mentre lavoriamo nella scienza, non c'è formula magica per come lo facciamo. La nostra più grande soluzione scientifica è la gente di talento che lavora insieme, ideando idee che aiutano le imprese ad aiutare le persone. In cambio, lasciamo che la nostra gente possieda le loro carriere. Le loro idee, grandi e piccole, migliorano veramente il mondo. E questo è il tipo di lavoro che vogliamo fare. Il nostro ingegnere del bioprocesso USP prenderà il controllo e gestirà le attività operative di progetti di produzione assegnati con la responsabilità complessiva di apparecchiature/sistemi di standardizzazione a Up Stream- (USP) all'interno della Business Unit, Manufacturing Complex 2 (6x20kL Large Scale Mammalian). Ogni giorno, i prodotti e i servizi di Lonza hanno un impatto positivo su milioni di persone. Per noi, questo non è solo un grande privilegio, ma anche una grande responsabilità. Come raggiungere i nostri risultati aziendali è altrettanto importante quanto i risultati stessi. A Lonza, rispettiamo e proteggiamo la nostra gente e il nostro ambiente. Qualsiasi successo ottenuto non è affatto successo se non raggiunto eticamente. La gente viene a Lonza per la sfida e la creatività di risolvere problemi complessi e sviluppare nuove idee nelle scienze della vita. In cambio, offriamo la soddisfazione che viene con migliorare le vite in tutto il mondo. La soddisfazione che viene con fare una differenza significativa. Lonza è leader globale nella produzione di farmaci e biotecnologie. L'azienda gestisce importanti stabilimenti produttivi a Visp (Valais), Basilea e Stein (Aargau), Svizzera. ## Mansioni - Sviluppa e implementa la strategia di standardizzazione MC2 e la roadmap in linea con la strategia locale (sito Visp) e la strategia globale Lonza SU. - Lead team di progetto multidisciplinari come lead funzionale per tutti gli aspetti produttivi dei progetti assegnati. - Assicurare un elevato livello di standardizzazione tecnica e di processo all'interno dell'impianto di produzione. - Lavorare come esperto di produzione in progetti di investimento di capitale per la costruzione e la messa in servizio di unità di produzione. - Implementare documenti GMP e requisiti normativi per i relativi beni e prodotti SU. ## Requisiti - BSC/MSc plus rispettivamente > 5/3 anni’ esperienza nell’industria delle biotecnologie. - Eccellente competenza biotecnologica e/o conoscenza nell'ingegneria biotecnica preferibile nell'ambito della cultura cellulare mammifero e delle operazioni di cattura primaria. - Esperienza pratica con acciaio inossidabile e sistemi di uso singolo. - Forte leadership e ottime capacità di comunicazione attraverso l'interazione con tutti i tipi di interfacce all'interno e all'esterno dell'organizzazione; forte orientamento del team. - Capacità di risoluzione dei problemi strutturati (Lean Six Sigma Yellow / Green Belt), atteggiamento di lavoro focalizzato e ben organizzato; mentalità aperta per nuove idee e suggerimenti; motivazione elevata e unità dinamica; orientata alla soluzione. - Sistemi esperti Delta V e/o Syncade Emerson. - Deviazione / indagini tra cui RCA - Change Management - Fluent in tedesco e/o inglese; comprensione GMP avanzata.
descriptionByLocale.en: Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across three continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of. The Bioprocess Engineer (BPE) provides technical and operational support for GMP manufacturing operations in Buffer & Media Preparation (BMS) Acting as the process Subject Matter Expert (SME), the BPE ensures safe, compliant, and reliable execution of manufacturing activities while driving standardization, continuous improvement, and operational readiness. The role works closely with Manufacturing, QA, MSAT, Engineering, and Automation, and maintains a strong shop‑floor presence during routine operations, PPQ, and critical GMP campaigns. Key Responsibilities Operational & Technical Support • Provide hands‑on technical support to manufacturing teams during GMP operations and PPQ campaigns • Act as process SME for assigned equipment, systems, and manufacturing activities • Support tier meetings and structured escalation of operational risks and issues • Participate in on‑call (pikett) support as required Quality & GMP Compliance • Lead or support deviation investigations, root cause analysis, and action closure • Ensure operations comply with cGMP, data integrity, and safety requirements • Author and review GMP documentation (SOPs, protocols, risk assessments, changes) • Support regulatory inspections and audit readiness activities Process Ownership & Improvement • Own assigned processes, recipes, and equipment from readiness through routine use • Drive standardization and process robustness across BMS • Support commissioning, qualification, and validation activities as manufacturing SME • Identify and implement continuous improvement opportunities (Lean / Six Sigma) Execution Readiness & Collaboration • Partner with Production and Planning to ensure material, equipment, and recipe readiness • Proactively identify and manage execution risks impacting batch success and schedule adherence • Collaborate cross‑functionally to resolve technical and compliance issues efficiently Required Qualifications • BSc or MSc in Biotechnology, Chemical Engineering, Bioprocess Engineering, or related field • Experience in GMP biopharmaceutical manufacturing (large‑scale operations preferred) • Strong understanding of BMS, USP, DSP, and/or single‑use systems • Working knowledge of GMP documentation, deviations, and investigations Key Competencies • Strong shop‑floor presence and problem‑solving capability • Structured, detail‑oriented, and quality‑focused mindset • Excellent communication and cross‑functional collaboration skills • Ability to prioritize and perform in a dynamic manufacturing environment What We Offer • Work in a state‑of‑the‑art large‑scale manufacturing environment • High‑impact role supporting PPQ and commercial production • Collaborative, safety‑first culture with strong development opportunities Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically. People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a meaningful difference. Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.
descriptionByLocale.de: Heute ist Lonza weltweit führend in Life Sciences, die auf drei Kontinenten operieren. Während wir in der Wissenschaft arbeiten, gibt es keine magische Formel, wie wir es tun. Unsere größte wissenschaftliche Lösung ist talentierte Menschen, die zusammenarbeiten und Ideen entwickeln, die Unternehmen helfen, Menschen zu helfen. Im Gegenzug lassen wir unsere Leute ihre Karriere besitzen. Ihre Ideen, groß und klein, wirklich verbessern die Welt. Und das ist die Art von Arbeit, von der wir teilnehmen wollen. Unser Bioprozess-Ingenieur USP übernimmt und verwaltet die operativen Aktivitäten von zugewiesenen Fertigungsprojekten mit Gesamtverantwortung von Standardisierungsanlagen/Systemen bei Up Stream- (USP) innerhalb der Business Unit, Manufacturing Complex 2 (6x20kL Large Scale Mammalian). Die Produkte und Dienstleistungen von Lonza haben jeden Tag einen positiven Einfluss auf Millionen von Menschen. Für uns ist dies nicht nur ein großes Privileg, sondern auch eine große Verantwortung. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erzielen, ist ebenso wichtig wie die Leistungen selbst. In Lonza respektieren und schützen wir unser Volk und unsere Umwelt. Jeder Erfolg, den wir erreichen, ist überhaupt kein Erfolg, wenn nicht ethisch erreicht. Die Menschen kommen nach Lonza für die Herausforderung und Kreativität, komplexe Probleme zu lösen und neue Ideen in den Life Sciences zu entwickeln. Im Gegenzug bieten wir die Zufriedenheit, die mit der Verbesserung des Lebens auf der ganzen Welt kommt. Die Zufriedenheit, die mit einem sinnvollen Unterschied kommt. Lonza ist weltweit führend in der Pharma- und Biotech-Produktion. Das Unternehmen betreibt große Produktionsstätten in Visp (Valais), Basel und Stein (Aargau), Schweiz. Â Mansioni - Entwickeln und implementieren MC2-Standardisierungsausrüstungsstrategie und Roadmap in Abstimmung mit lokaler (Site Visp) und globaler Lonza SU-Strategie. - Führen Sie multidisziplinäre Projektteams als Funktionsleiter für alle Fertigungsaspekte von zugewiesenen Projekten. - Gewährleistung einer hohen technischen und verfahrenstechnischen Standardisierung innerhalb der Produktionsanlage. - Arbeit als Produktionsexperte in Investitionsprojekten für den Bau und die Inbetriebnahme von Fertigungseinheiten. - Ergänzend zum Stand der Technik GMP-Dokumente und regulatorische Anforderungen an entsprechende SU-Aktiva und -Produkte. Requisiti - BSC/MSc plus bzw. > 5/3 Jahre Erfahrung in der Biotechnologieindustrie. - Ausgezeichnete Biotechnologie-Know-how und/oder Kenntnisse in der biotechnischen Technik vorzuziehen im Bereich der Säugetierzellenkultur und der Primäreinfang. - Praktische Erfahrung mit Edelstahl und Single Use Systems. - Starke Führung und sehr gute Kommunikationsfähigkeiten durch Interaktion mit allen Arten von Schnittstellen innerhalb und außerhalb der Organisation; starke Teamorientierung. - Strukturierte Problemlösung Fähigkeiten (Lean Six Sigma Yellow / Green Belt), fokussierte und gut organisierte Arbeitshaltung; offen für neue Ideen und Anregungen; hohe Motivation und dynamische Antriebs; lösungsorientiert. - Expert Delta V und/oder Syncade Emerson Systeme. - Abweichung / Untersuchungen einschließlich RCA - Change Management - Fluent in Deutsch und/oder Englisch; fortgeschrittenes GMP-Verstehen.
descriptionByLocale.fr: Descrizione Aujourd'hui, Lonza est un leader mondial des sciences de la vie opérant sur trois continents. Alors que nous travaillons dans la science, il n'y a pas de formule magique pour la façon dont nous le faisons. Notre plus grande solution scientifique est de travailler ensemble avec des gens talentueux, en concevant des idées qui aident les entreprises à aider les gens. En échange, nous laissons notre peuple posséder sa carrière. Leurs idées, grandes et petites, améliorent véritablement le monde. Et c'est le genre de travail dont nous voulons faire partie. Notre ingénieur en bioprocédés USP prendra en charge et gérera les activités opérationnelles des projets de fabrication assignés avec la responsabilité globale de l'équipement/systèmes de normalisation à Up Stream- (USP) au sein de l'unité d'affaires, Complexe de fabrication 2 (6x20kL Mammalien à grande échelle). Chaque jour, les produits et services de Lonza ont un impact positif sur des millions de personnes. Pour nous, ce n'est pas seulement un grand privilège, mais aussi une grande responsabilité. La façon dont nous obtenons nos résultats commerciaux est tout aussi importante que les réalisations elles-mêmes. À Lonza, nous respectons et protégeons notre peuple et notre environnement. Tout succès que nous obtenons n'est pas du tout un succès sinon un succès éthique. Les gens viennent à Lonza pour le défi et la créativité de résoudre des problèmes complexes et de développer de nouvelles idées dans les sciences de la vie. En retour, nous offrons la satisfaction qui vient avec l'amélioration de la vie partout dans le monde. La satisfaction qui vient avec faire une différence significative. Lonza est un leader mondial dans la fabrication de produits pharmaceutiques et de biotechnologies. La société exploite d'importantes installations de production à Viège (Valais), Bâle et Stein (Argovie), Suisse. ## Mansioni - Élaborer et mettre en oeuvre une stratégie et une feuille de route en matière d'équipement de normalisation MC2 en harmonie avec la stratégie locale (site Viège) et la stratégie mondiale Lonza SU. - Diriger les équipes de projets multidisciplinaires comme chef de file fonctionnel pour tous les aspects manufacturiers des projets assignés. - Assurer un niveau élevé de normalisation technique et des procédés dans l'usine de production. - Travailler en tant qu'expert de la production dans les projets d'investissement en capital pour la construction et la mise en service d'unités de fabrication. - Mettre en œuvre les documents de pointe sur les BPF et les exigences réglementaires pour les actifs et produits SU correspondants. ## Requisiti - BSC/MSc plus respectivement > 5/3 ans d'expérience dans l'industrie de la biotechnologie. - Excellente expertise en biotechnologie et/ou connaissances en génie biotechnique préférable dans le domaine de la culture cellulaire des mammifères et des opérations de capture primaire. - Expérience pratique en acier inoxydable et systèmes à usage unique. - Un leadership solide et de très bonnes compétences en communication grâce à l'interaction avec toutes sortes d'interfaces à l'intérieur et à l'extérieur de l'organisation; une forte orientation de l'équipe. - Compétences structurées en résolution de problèmes (Lean Six Sigma Yellow / Green Belt), attitude de travail bien organisée et ciblée; ouverture d'esprit pour de nouvelles idées et suggestions; motivation élevée et dynamisme; orientation vers la solution. - Systèmes Delta V et/ou Syncade Emerson. - Déviation / enquêtes incluant RCA - Gestion du changement - Fluent en allemand et/ou en anglais; compréhension avancée des BPF.

jobs.title: Bioprocess Engineer
jobs.description:
Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across three continents. While we work in science, there is no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that is the kind of work we want to be part of.

Our Bioprocess Engineer USP will take over and manage the operational activities of assigned manufacturing projects with overall responsibility of standardization equipment/systems at Up Stream- (USP) within the Business Unit, Manufacturing Complex 2 (6x20kL Large Scale Mammalian). 

Key responsibilities:

• Develop and implement MC2 standardization equipment strategy and roadmap in alignment with local (site Visp) and global Lonza SU strategy.

• Lead multi-disciplinary project teams as functional lead for all manufacturing aspects of assigned projects.

• Ensure a high level of technical and process standardization within the production plant.

• Work as production expert in capital investment projects for building and commissioning of manufacturing units.

• Implement state of the art GMP documents and regulatory requirements for corresponding SU assets and products. 

Key requirements:

• BSC/MSc plus respectively > 5/3 years’ experience within the biotechnology industry.

• Excellent biotechnology expertise and/or knowledge in biotechnical engineering preferable in the area of mammalian cell culture and primary capture operations.

• Practical experience with stainless steel and Single Use Systems.

• Strong leadership and very good communication skills through interaction with all kinds of interfaces within and outside the organization; strong team orientation. 

• Structured problem solving skills (Lean Six Sigma Yellow / Green Belt), focused and well-organized working attitude; open-minded for new ideas and suggestions; high motivation and dynamic drive; solution-oriented.

• Expert Delta V and/or Syncade Emerson systems.

• Deviation / investigations including RCA

• Change Management

• Fluent in German and/or English; advanced GMP understanding.

Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically. 
People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a meaningful difference.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

descriptionByLocale.en:
## Descrizione
Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across three continents. While we work in science, there is no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that is the kind of work we want to be part of.

## Mansioni
- Develop and implement MC2 standardization equipment strategy and roadmap in alignment with local (site Visp) and global Lonza SU strategy.
- Lead multi-disciplinary project teams as functional lead for all manufacturing aspects of assigned projects.
- Ensure a high level of technical and process standardization within the production plant.
- Work as production expert in capital investment projects for building and commissioning of manufacturing units.
- Implement state of the art GMP documents and regulatory requirements for corresponding SU assets and products.

## Requisiti
- BSC/MSc plus respectively > 5/3 years’ experience within the biotechnology industry.
- Excellent biotechnology expertise and/or knowledge in biotechnical engineering preferable in the area of mammalian cell culture and primary capture operations.
- Practical experience with stainless steel and Single Use Systems.
- Strong leadership and very good communication skills through interaction with all kinds of interfaces within and outside the organization; strong team orientation.
- Structured problem solving skills (Lean Six Sigma Yellow / Green Belt), focused and well-organized working attitude; open-minded for new ideas and suggestions; high motivation and dynamic drive; solution-oriented.
- Expert Delta V and/or Syncade Emerson systems.
- Deviation / investigations including RCA
- Change Management
- Fluent in German and/or English; advanced GMP understanding.

## Cosa offriamo
Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically.
People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a meaningful difference.

## Contatto
Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

Pour nous, ce n'est pas seulement un grand privilège, mais aussi une grande responsabilité. La façon dont nous obtenons nos résultats commerciaux est tout aussi importante que les réalisations elles-mêmes. À Lonza, nous respectons et protégeons notre peuple et notre environnement. Tout succès que nous obtenons n'est pas du tout un succès sinon un succès éthique. Les gens viennent à Lonza pour le défi et la créativité de résoudre des problèmes complexes et de développer de nouvelles idées dans les sciences de la vie. En retour, nous offrons la satisfaction qui vient avec l'amélioration de la vie partout dans le monde. La satisfaction qui vient avec faire une différence significative. ## Contatto Lonza est un leader mondial dans la fabrication de produits pharmaceutiques et de biotechnologies. La société exploite d'importantes installations de production à Viège (Valais), Bâle et Stein (Argovie), Suisse.

jobs.title: Senior Scientist, Equipment Qualification (m/f/d) 80-100%
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Stein, AG, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

Lead the GMP-compliant qualification and re‑qualification of a great variety of analytical instruments and software in our QC laboratory. At our growing site, this position provides exceptional opportunities to learn, contribute, and make a meaningful impact. Check out your career in Quality Control!

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.

• Benefits in Stein: https://bit.ly/42DlBDk 

What you will do:

• Lead and manage the initial qualification and re-qualification of analytical equipment and software in our QC laboratory (HPLC, CE, refrigerators etc.)

• Execution of performance qualification and act as a trainer for defined equipment and related processes

• Author, review and approve documentation (qualification plans and reports; documents from service providers) related to laboratory systems as required

• Ensure compliance of laboratory systems according to cGMP and current standard processes and regulatory guidelines as well as respective SOP`s.

• Oversight and guidance for proper maintenance and qualified state of equipment; Performing period review of qualification/calibration of analytical equipment and act as system owner for defined Equipment

• Participate as Subject Matter Expert in audits and inspections

What we are looking for:

• Solid work experience in the GMP-controlled Quality Control laboratory is a must

• Hands-on experience in qualification of analytical instruments is a must

• Apprenticeship or academical degree in Analytical Chemistry, Biochemistry, Pharmaceutical Sciences or related field

• Broad experience in analytical methodologies like e.g. HPLC, ICief, UV-VIS, Elisa, CE-SDS is an advantage. Knowledge of lab quality systems like LIMS, Tiamo or Empower/Chromeleon is a plus

• Fluency in written and spoken English is a must, Language skills in German is a plus

• You enjoy working independently and in a dynamic & fast-pacing project environment

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.
Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

Applichi subito. Lonza è leader globale nella produzione di farmaci e biotecnologie. jobs.title: Line Mechanic (f/m/d)
jobs.description:
Location. Visp.

Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across three continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of.

What you’ll get:

• An agile career and dynamic working culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

• A variety of benefits dependant on role and location.

The full list of our global benefits can be also found on https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits.

Your tasks:

• Repairing or replacing malfunctioning components, parts, and other mechanical or electrical equipment in the clean room and sterile environment (experience with aseptic filling lines is preferrable)  

• Performing functional tests to validate the effectiveness of repair work. Maintaining records of maintenance services and repairs

• Executes manufacturing activities in the area of Drug Product according to cGMP guidelines, ensuring batch execution, evaluating test results, resolving issues, troubleshoot manufacturing equipment and make recommendations for resolution

• Eliminating faults in the systems

• Open and track malfunction reports

• Continuous improvements

• Cooperate, advice and support Plant Engineering department

• Coordination of work processes in consultation with the team leaders

• Advanced handling of SAP, Cornerstone, DMS, Office 365

Your profile:

• You have completed an EFZ apprenticeship as a mechanic or related profession (production mechanic, car mechanic, etc.)

• Several years of experience as a mechanic is an advantage

• Excellent interpretation of P&ID diagrams or drawings

• Very good knowledge of German is essential, English is an advantage

• Independent and precise way of working

• You are customer-oriented and open to new ideas

• Communication skills, initiative and resilience round off your profile.

• Willing to work in 3-Shifts pattern.

Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically.

People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a meaningful difference.

Lonza is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, status as a qualified individual with disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

descriptionByLocale.it: Descrizione Location. Visp. Oggi, Lonza è leader globale nelle scienze della vita che operano in tre continenti. Mentre lavoriamo nella scienza, non c'è formula magica per come lo facciamo. La nostra più grande soluzione scientifica è la gente di talento che lavora insieme, ideando idee che aiutano le imprese ad aiutare le persone. In cambio, lasciamo che la nostra gente possieda le loro carriere. Le loro idee, grandi e piccole, migliorano veramente il mondo. E questo è il tipo di lavoro di cui vogliamo essere parte. # Cosa# offrendo - Una carriera agile e una cultura di lavoro dinamica. - No. Un luogo di lavoro inclusivo ed etico. - Programmi di compensazione che riconoscono alte prestazioni. - Una varietà di vantaggi dipende dal ruolo e dalla posizione. L'elenco completo dei nostri benefici globali è disponibile anche su https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits. ## Mansioni - Riparazione o sostituzione di componenti, parti e altre apparecchiature meccaniche o elettriche nella stanza pulita e nell'ambiente sterile (l'esperienza con le linee di riempimento asettiche è preferibile) - Eseguire test funzionali per convalidare l'efficacia del lavoro di riparazione. Mantenere registri di servizi di manutenzione e riparazioni - Esegui attività di produzione nell'area di Drug Product secondo le linee guida cGMP, assicurando l'esecuzione in batch, valutando i risultati di test, risolvendo i problemi, risolvere le attrezzature di produzione e fare raccomandazioni per la risoluzione - Eliminare i difetti nei sistemi - rapporti di malfunzionamento aperto e traccia - Miglioramenti continui - Coopera, consulenza e supporto reparto di ingegneria vegetale - Coordinamento dei processi di lavoro in consultazione con i dirigenti del team - Formazione avanzata - Formazione professionale - Formazione avanzata - Formazione professionale - Diversi anni di esperienza come meccanico è un vantaggio - Ottima interpretazione di diagrammi o disegni P&ID - Molto buona conoscenza del tedesco è essenziale, l'inglese è un vantaggio - Indipendente e preciso modo di lavorare - Sei orientato al cliente e aperto a nuove idee - Capacità di comunicazione, iniziativa e resilienza completano il tuo profilo. - Volontà di lavorare nel modello 3-Shifts. ## Informazioni aggiuntive Ogni giorno, i prodotti e i servizi di Lonza hanno un impatto positivo su milioni di persone. Per noi, questo non è solo un grande privilegio, ma anche una grande responsabilità. Come raggiungere i nostri risultati aziendali è altrettanto importante quanto i risultati stessi. A Lonza, rispettiamo e proteggiamo la nostra gente e il nostro ambiente. Qualsiasi successo ottenuto non è affatto successo se non raggiunto eticamente. La gente viene a Lonza per la sfida e la creatività di risolvere problemi complessi e sviluppare nuove idee nelle scienze della vita. In cambio, offriamo la soddisfazione che viene con migliorare le vite in tutto il mondo. La soddisfazione che viene con fare una differenza significativa. Lonza è un datore di lavoro pari opportunità. Tutti i candidati qualificati riceveranno considerazione per l'occupazione senza riguardo a razza, religione, colore, origine nazionale, sesso, orientamento sessuale, identità di genere, età, stato come individuo qualificato con disabilità, status veterano protetto, o qualsiasi altra caratteristica protetta dalla legge. Lonza è leader globale nella produzione di farmaci e biotecnologie. L'azienda gestisce importanti stabilimenti produttivi a Visp (Valais), Basilea e Stein (Aargau), Svizzera. ## Contatto Nessuna informazione di contatto fornita.
